S.text: Yeah, first time. I've worn glasses since, since I was twelve.
It doesn't really feel anything's in there.
So the trial one's not quite right?
How long can I wear them a day?

O.text: So your spectacle prescription, let me pull it up — right eye is -3.75, sorry, -3.50 sphere, with a -0.75 cylinder at axis 170.
Left eye is -4.25 sphere, no cylinder to speak of.
For a first fit I'm going to start with a standard soft lens, base curve 8.6, diameter fourteen0.2.
He's had them settling for about, ten minutes now.
Okay — centration is good on the right, the lens is covering the cornea fully, and I'm seeing about, 0.3 millimeters of movement on the blink.
Left eye, same thing — well centered, good coverage, movement looks maybe a touch more, but within range.

Now let me do an over-refraction to check the power on the eye. chart is up. The trial is close; the over-refraction just tells me the final power. Over the left lens you're already 20/20, no over-refraction needed there. Base curve eight six, diameter fourteen two for both. I'll grab the practice lenses and the case.

A.text: Successful first soft contact lens fit OU; over-refraction refined OD power.

P.text: So final contact lens power: right eye -4.00— -4.00, left eye -4.25.
I'll have the tech teach you insertion and removal, and we'll do a one-week follow-up to check the fit with wear.
Start with, let's say, six to eight hours a day this first week, then we'll build up.
Come back in a week and we'll check the health of the eye and confirm the prescription.
